evidence: Developing, testing, and deploying resource-intensive AI systems may require additional investment and increase our costs.

We maintain AI governance, including policies and a council, incorporating AI into our Nth Party Risk Assessment process, and implementing technical controls that enable people to use AI to complete tasks more efficiently.
AI, including Generative AI and Post-Quantum Cryptography, has the potential to change the way we operate by creating efficiencies and improving processes and customer experiences.
We maintain AI governance, including policies and a council, incorporating AI into our Nth Party Risk Assessment process, and implementing technical controls.
We are exploring opportunities for the potential sale of power from our nuclear facilities pursuant to long-term agreements with large power users, such as data centers.
New types of cyberattacks may be generated or enhanced through the use of AI and/or cloud-based infrastructure. A successful cybersecurity attack may result in unauthorized use of our systems.

AI algorithms that we or our Nth-party vendors use may be flawed or may be based on data sets that are biased or insufficient. These limitations could result in reputational damage, unauthorized disclosure of data, and legal liabilities.
Evolving nature of AI may cause new laws and regulations to be enacted which may require significant resources to modify and maintain business practices to comply.

limitations: No quantified AI-attributable dollar amounts, headcount, or capex figures appear in the excerpts; investment intensity capped at 3.
AI use cases are named (governance council, cybersecurity controls, efficiency tools) but no deployment metrics or quantified outcomes are disclosed, capping concreteness at 3.

Risk disclosure is company-specific with named mechanisms (vendor AI flaws, regulatory change, cyberattack enhancement via AI, data breach) but no quantification of financial exposure or formal risk-quantification program disclosed, placing depth at 4 rather than 5.
Excerpts are pre-filtered; additional AI investment or use-case detail may exist elsewhere in the filing.
